name: Coinstar Kiosk
description_short: Coinstar operates self-service kiosks offering coin-to-cash conversion, cryptocurrency purchases, Amazon Cash loading, and account transfers at retail locations nationwide.
description_long:
Coinstar is a long-established operator of self-service kiosks found in grocery stores and retail locations across the United States. The company was recently acquired by Arctic Slope Regional Corporation (ASRC) as of February 2026. Coinstar's primary service is converting loose coins into cash within minutes without requiring customers to sort, count, or roll their coins—a convenience-focused alternative to traditional bank coin counting.

Beyond coin conversion, Coinstar kiosks now offer a diverse suite of financial and cryptocurrency services. Customers can exchange coins for eGift Cards (with some fee-free options), donate coins to participating charities, load cash to Amazon accounts ($5-$500), purchase cryptocurrencies via partnerships with Coinme and CINQ by Coinstar (powered by Zero Hash), transfer funds to checking accounts, and obtain CINQ by Coinstar debit cards. The company emphasizes accessibility by placing kiosks in convenient retail locations rather than requiring bank visits.

Coinstar distinguishes itself through its multi-service kiosk model and cryptocurrency integration. Rather than being a single-purpose coin exchange operator, the platform has evolved to include modern financial services like crypto purchasing and digital wallet functionality. The availability of no-fee eGift Card options and charitable donation pathways also differentiate the service. Amazon Cash integration provides an alternative for unbanked or underbanked consumers to shop online without credit cards.

The honest assessment is that while Coinstar provides genuine convenience for coin conversion and offers legitimate access to alternative financial services, fees vary significantly by location and service. Cryptocurrency services carry inherent volatility risks. The kiosk model, while convenient, may not provide the customer service depth of traditional financial institutions. Consumers should compare location-specific fees and understand that services vary by individual kiosk—not all locations offer all services listed.
pros: Coin-to-cash conversion requires no sorting, counting, or rolling—completed in minutes at retail grocery store locations
No-fee eGift Card exchange options available, reducing conversion costs for certain redemptions
Multiple cash conversion pathways including Amazon Cash ($5-$500), charity donations, and account transfers
Cryptocurrency purchasing integrated directly (Coinme and CINQ by Coinstar) with 25+ crypto options available
Account transfer service allows customers to deposit coins or cash directly to checking accounts, bypassing bank lines
Charitable giving option lets customers donate change to participating nonprofits through Coins that Count program
CINQ by Coinstar debit card and digital wallet provide unbanked/underbanked consumers with payment tools
cons: Fees vary significantly by location and service type, with no transparent upfront fee disclosure on main website
Not all services available at every kiosk—website shows individual locations have different service menus
Cryptocurrency services carry full market volatility risk; CINQ and Coinme do not protect against crypto price fluctuations
Bill acceptance explicitly unavailable at many locations (website notes 'Accepts Bills: N' for featured kiosk)
Foreign currency exchange explicitly not offered, limiting international traveler utility
best_for: Consumers with accumulated loose coins seeking quick, no-hassle cash conversion at convenient retail locations
Unbanked or underbanked individuals wanting to load Amazon accounts or access cryptocurrency without traditional banking relationships
Charity donors who want to convert spare change into tax-deductible donations to participating nonprofits
Budget-conscious shoppers seeking fee-free or low-fee eGift Card redemptions as an alternative to direct cash
diagnosis: Coinstar kiosks are best for consumers with spare coins who value convenience and want quick cash conversion at retail locations, or unbanked/underbanked individuals seeking alternative financial services like cryptocurrency access or Amazon Cash. The main caveat is that fees vary by location and service, not all services appear at every kiosk, and cryptocurrency services carry full market risk—consumers must verify fees and available services at their specific location before visiting.
services: eGift Card exchange from coin conversion (brands and retailers vary by location)
Coins that Count charity donation program
Amazon Cash loading ($5-$500 to Amazon account balance)
CINQ by Coinstar cryptocurrency exchange (25+ cryptocurrency options)
CINQ by Coinstar digital wallet and debit card
Transfer to Account (deposit coins or cash directly to checking accounts)
Financial institution partner transfers (fees vary by partner bank)
